_meta.source: Bootstrapped from contractor sample BOM 'Lot 1 T075 Elm ACL BOM.xls' (22 line items). Industry-standard quantity rules per Richard's note that ProCalcs follows standard practice — refine as real projects expose edge cases.

fields.sku: supplier-specific part number, used as the primary key
fields.supplier: GOODMAN | PRJ | PGM | WSF | RHEA — 'src' column on the contractor sheet
fields.section: Equipment | Duct System Equipment | Rheia Duct System Equipment | Labor — output grouping
fields.phase: Rough | Finish | null — only Rheia uses this today
fields.description: human-readable line shown in the BOM
fields.trigger: structured rule the engine evaluates against design_data; see _trigger_keys below
fields.default_unit_price: USD per unit; 0 means 'price not set, must come from contractor profile or supplier API'
fields.notes: free-form for the engineer maintaining this catalog

_trigger_keys.heat_kit_present: AHU is electric and a strip-heat kit is required
_trigger_keys.rheia_in_scope: small-diameter flexible duct (≤4") detected anywhere in duct_runs OR raw_rup_context — Rheia is universal across ProCalcs projects per Tom 2026-04-29
_trigger_keys.round_vinyl_duct: any round vinyl duct in scope (typically transitions / short runs)

_quantity_modes.fixed: { mode: 'fixed', value: N }                        — emit N every time the trigger fires
_quantity_modes.per_unit: { mode: 'per_unit', source: 'equipment.ahu' }      — N per matching item
_quantity_modes.per_lf: { mode: 'per_lf', source: 'duct_runs.rectangular' } — sum LF of matching duct runs
_quantity_modes.rheia_per_lf: { mode: 'rheia_per_lf', divisor: N }                — Rheia 3-in duct LF total, optionally divided

items.notes: Sample BOM shipped 1 unit. Real selection should depend on AHU tonnage from RUP equipment table — this SKU is for one specific tonnage; need a tonnage→SKU map.
Same tonnage-mapping caveat as AHU. Sample shipped 1 unit.
1 ERV per residence is typical for ADUs / small-residential. Larger jobs may have multiple.
Sized to AHU; 5 kW is the most common size for ADU-scale ATUs. Larger AHUs need bigger kits — needs SKU variants.
Sample BOM had 4.12 LF for one trunk. Quantity is total LF across all rectangular runs. Size (17x12) varies by tonnage; needs a size→SKU map.

items.description: Plenum round take off with trans, size 17" x 12"
items.notes: 1 plenum take-off per AHU. Size matched to AHU plenum dimensions.
Sample shipped 3 units. Used for short transitions; default 3 is a guess — refine when more samples available.
Sample shipped 2 units. Maps to ceiling round registers. Size pair (12x12-10) varies by register face / duct diameter.

items.description: Rectangular metal high wall register, 24" x 4"
items.notes: Sample shipped 6 units. High-wall rectangular face. Pricing zero in sample → use contractor profile.
Sample shipped 2 units. Used for return-air grills. Pricing zero in sample.
Total LF of 3-in flexible duct in scope. Sample shipped 856 LF — anchor expectation. Quantity comes from RUP duct_runs filtered to ≤3" diameter.
Sample 84 ferrules / 856 LF ≈ 1 per ~10 LF. Used to join sections; rule may also be 'per coupler + per take-off'. Refine after seeing 2–3 more samples.
Sample 27. Take-offs from main trunk into branches; one inside + one outside per branch (sample has 27/27 matched pairs).
Paired 1:1 with inside take-off above. Sample 27.
Sample 15 / 856 LF ≈ 1 per 57 LF. Joins duct sections at long-run splice points.

Sample 42. Should map directly to count of elbows in fittings array — but sample fittings array isn't populated by parser today. Could fall back to a heuristic (e.g. 1.5 per branch run) until parser fills it in.
Sample 17. Count of registers terminating in high-sidewall locations. Pairs 1:1 with slotted-diffuser SKU below.
Sample 17 — pairs with High Sidewall Boot Assembly above.
Sample 37 / 856 LF ≈ 1 per 23 LF. Likely placed only on trunk runs, not every branch. Refine after more samples — Rheia spec for flex is closer to 1 per 4–6 ft, so this may be trunk-only.
Sample 10. Pairs 1:1 with Ceiling Diffuser Assembly below.
Sample 10 — pairs with Ceiling Boot Assembly above. Total endpoints (high_sidewall 17 + ceiling 10 = 27) matches the take-off count, which is a useful internal consistency check.
